titleStmt.title: Letter from Thomas Patrick Gill to Sir Matthew Nathan, 6 May 1916
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: Thomas Patrick Gill

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p: A letter from Thomas Patrick Gill (1858-1931) to Sir Matthew Nathan (1862-1939). In the letter Gill states the he is transmitting statements of the movements of the department's staff between 22 April and 1 May, the week of the Easter Rising. Gill adds that it was not possible to contact all members of staff and any further statements will be sent on later.Sir Matthew Nathan was the under-secretary for Ireland from 1914 to 1916. In 1916 Nathan offered his resignation following the outbreak of the Easter Rising. Gill served as the secretary for the Department of Agriculture and Technical Instruction from its establishment in 1900 until 1923.

msIdentifier.collection: Chief Secretary's Office Registered Papers, CSO RP/1916/7721/2

correspAction.placeName: Department of Agriculture and Technical Instruction, Dublin, Ireland.

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
Department of Agriculture and Technical Instruction for Ireland, Upper Merrion Street, Dublin 6 May, 1916. Sir, With reference to your letter of the 1st
instant, I am desired by the Vice-President to
transmit, for the information of His Excellency
the Lord Lieutenant, the accompanying statements
as to the movements, &c., between April 22nd
and May 1st of all the members of the
Departmentâs staff employed in Dublin with whom
it has been possible to get in touch up to the
present. Any further statements will be
forwarded as received. I am,
Sir,
Your obedient Servant, T. P. Gill
Secretary. The Under Secretary, Dublin Castle. Chief Commissioner
8/5/16
